name: SHAP (SHapley Additive exPlanations)

performance.notes: TreeExplainer sub-millisecond for tree models; DeepExplainer and KernelExplainer much slower for neural networks

agent_readiness.notes: Industry-standard ML explainability library based on game theory (Shapley values). Explains why a model made a specific prediction. Explainers: TreeExplainer (fast, for XGBoost/LightGBM/sklearn), DeepExplainer (neural nets), KernelExplainer (model-agnostic). Visualizations: waterfall, beeswarm, bar, force plots. Feature importance, interaction values. Used in regulatory compliance, model debugging, and bias analysis.

gotchas: KernelExplainer is slow for large datasets or many features
DeepExplainer approximates — not exact for all architectures
SHAP values can be misleading with correlated features
Requires background dataset for most explainers
best_when: Understanding model predictions for regulatory compliance (banking, insurance, health)
Feature importance analysis for tree models (XGBoost, LightGBM, RandomForest)
Debugging model behavior and identifying prediction errors
Detecting bias and fairness issues in ML models
avoid_when: LLM explanations (use attention visualization or probing instead)
Teams needing simpler explanations (LIME may be more interpretable)
summary: SHAP is the industry standard for ML model explainability, based on Shapley values from game theory. Explains individual predictions for any model. TreeExplainer is extremely fast for XGBoost/LightGBM/scikit-learn models. Beautiful visualizations (waterfall, beeswarm). Used for regulatory compliance, model debugging, and bias detection. Most widely adopted XAI library in production ML.
